title: Update banner now catches new versions mid-session
description: Previously the banner checked latest.yml exactly once on mount, so a client sitting in the tray for hours never noticed new releases until it was restarted. Main process now polls latest.yml hourly and refreshes on main-window show/focus (with a 30s throttle so rapid window bounces don't thrash GitHub). When a newer version is found, an IPC event lights up the banner in whatever window is open.

title: Release workflow: -beta / -rc / -alpha tags become GitHub pre-releases
description: Tags like v1.29.4-beta.1 now get the --prerelease flag on gh release create, which keeps them out of releases/latest. Stable clients polling latest.yml won't see pre-releases; testers install the .exe manually from the pre-release page. Promoting to stable is a separate v1.29.4 tag without the suffix. First piece of the manual beta-channel workflow — no client-side channel logic yet, just server-side gating.

title: Windows auto-updater: "Connection error" when clicking Download
description: Auto-updater read latest.yml and fetched an installer URL with dashes (SmurovProxy-Setup-1.29.x.exe), but the actual asset on the GitHub release had dots (SmurovProxy.Setup.1.29.x.exe). electron-builder's default NSIS output puts spaces in the filename ("SmurovProxy Setup 1.29.x.exe"), which `gh release create` then uploads to GitHub, which normalizes spaces to dots in the asset name. Meanwhile latest.yml is generated by electron-builder with its own URL-safe normalization that uses dashes, so yml and the actual GitHub asset diverged — every Update click on Windows returned 404 and the banner showed "Connection error". Pinning nsis.artifactName to `${productName}-Setup-${version}.${ext}` makes the on-disk filename dashed; GitHub preserves dashes, so URL now matches latest.yml end to end. Existing v1.29.2 users were unblocked separately by renaming the release asset in place.

title: Kill stale daemon on startup before spawning a fresh one
description: If the Electron main process dies non-gracefully (force quit from Activity Monitor, kernel-level SIGKILL, crash without running before-quit), the child daemon process is orphaned and keeps running with whatever code version it was built from. Users who installed newer versions via PKG without explicitly quitting the old app ended up with a stale daemon from hours or days ago — no perf fixes, no bug fixes. startDaemon now probes 127.0.0.1:9090 before spawning: if something answers, it looks up the PID via lsof (macOS) or netstat (Windows) and SIGKILLs it, waits for the port to release, then starts the fresh binary.

title: TUN engine wedged in Reconnecting after UDP→TLS fallback + D3 force-close
description: Triggered by the 1.29.0 server redeploy: client's D1 caught the server session close, reconnected, UDP timed out and AutoTransport fell back to TLS, then D3 fired on accumulated stream failures and force-closed the TLS transport. TLSTransport had no DoneChan method, so AutoTransport.DoneChan returned nil, and the healthLoop's `<-doneCh` branch blocked forever on a nil channel — D1 never got the wake-up it was supposed to. D2 just counts failures without reconnecting, so after 60 seconds (12 × 5s) it exhausted and stopped the engine, leaving the user to press Connect manually. Two fixes: (1) TLSTransport now implements DoneChan + Alive to match UDPTransport so AutoTransport delegation works uniformly; (2) D3 no longer relies on Close-then-DoneChan to trigger D1 — it calls reconnectTransport directly, mirroring D1's own recovery path.

title: Mode selector in the header, traffic switch on the main tab
description: Full (TUN) / Browser only toggle is now always visible in the title bar, and the All traffic / Selected apps switch lives inline with the Main tab.

title: Landing page rebuilt
description: proxy.smurov.com — new hero, six feature cards (port 443, AutoTransport, hybrid TUN+Browser, split tunneling, Kill Switch, hardware-bound), benchmarks vs WireGuard and Outline, three-step onboarding, EN/RU language switcher with localStorage persistence, admin panel link removed, download buttons resolve to the latest GitHub release with proper Apple and Windows SVG logos.

title: Windows startup crash since 1.27 — loader-to-main window handoff
description: Create the main window before destroying the loader. The old order destroyed the loader first, which fired window-all-closed in the same tick, and on Windows that handler quit the app before createWindow could finish. macOS was unaffected because window-all-closed is a no-op there.

title: Windows daemon idle CPU went from 40-70% to 0-1%
description:
A multi-week pprof hunt that uncovered nine independent bugs in the Windows packet path. Each fix unblocked the next one. Highlights:

- Physical interface cache was a no-op stub on Windows. Every protectedDial enumerated network adapters via GetAdaptersAddresses, which dominated CPU and GC.
- procinfo cache forced a full kernel TCP/UDP table scan on every miss, thrashing on browsers cycling ephemeral ports.
- IP_UNICAST_IF byte order was wrong (`<<16` instead of `<<24` / htonl) — Windows silently ignored the bogus value, "bypass" sockets followed the TUN default route, and the daemon's own packets looped back through bridgeInbound.
- UDP and TLS transports were using net.DialUDP / tls.DialWithDialer directly with no interface binding. Even after the byte-order fix, IP_UNICAST_IF turned out to be only "advisory" on connected UDP sockets — fix was binding the socket source IP via Dialer.LocalAddr to the physical interface.
- bridgeInbound and bridgeOutbound were allocating one fresh slice per packet, generating ~67 GB of garbage in 30 seconds. Reused buffers, dropped GC pressure 30×.
- bridgeOutbound silently died on the first packet because `Buffer.ReadAt` returns io.EOF on a successful full read (io.ReaderAt convention) and the new implementation treated that as fatal.
- NAT readLoop held a 64 KB buffer per entry for 60 seconds. Browsers doing hundreds of DNS lookups while watching YouTube ended up with 1 GB resident in NAT alone. Buffer shrunk to 2 KB, pooled via sync.Pool, default TTL dropped to 10s.

title: NAT readLoop captured aliased IP slices into a reused packet buffer
description: After the bridgeInbound buffer-reuse perf fix, NAT.HandlePacket was passing srcIP/dstIP slices that aliased the caller's buffer straight into a goroutine that outlived the call. After bridgeInbound moved to the next packet, the goroutine's stored IPs became garbage — reply UDP packets (DNS responses, voice) were being built with bogus headers and silently dropped. Browsers stayed working because PAC sites go through SOCKS5 (server-side resolve), but Telegram, Discord, and anything depending on local DNS broke. Both IPs are now cloned before the readLoop spawns.

title: Synchronous reentrancy guard in client startReconnect
description: The previous guard checked the React `reconnecting` state, which updates async — when the polling effect and a transport-error effect fired in the same tick, both saw `reconnecting=false` and spawned independent retry loops. Result: parallel /tun/start calls hammering the daemon with "engine already active, restarting" and rebuilding gVisor stack and NAT tables. Now uses a synchronous ref so only one retry loop runs at a time.

title: Diagnostic crash logger and pprof endpoints
description: Daemon exposes /debug/pprof/* on its local 127.0.0.1:9090 API for CPU/heap/alloc profiling. Main process has an opt-in crash logger behind `--trace` (or SMUROV_DEBUG=1 on macOS — Windows requireAdministrator drops env vars on the elevated child, so use the flag there) that captures unhandled exceptions, renderer crashes, and per-phase boot traces to ~/Desktop/smurov-crash.log. Engine.Start now dumps a goroutine stack trace if it has to stop a still-active engine, so runaway client retry loops are immediately diagnosable.

title: NAT readLoop memory bloat — 1 GB resident, 60% CPU on idle browsing
description: Heap profile from 1.28.16 showed 1052 MB (96% of all live memory) held in NATTable.readLoop's per-entry buffers. Each NAT entry spawned a goroutine with `make([]byte, 65535)`, and entries lived for 60s — so a browser doing hundreds of DNS lookups while watching YouTube ended up with thousands of live entries × 64 KB. The matching alloc_space profile showed 62 GB cumulative through the same path, which is why the GC was running so hard. Three changes: (1) shrink the read buffer from 65 535 to 2 048 bytes — covers any realistic MTU + headroom, drops per-entry footprint by 32×, (2) recycle the buffers via sync.Pool so dial bursts don't allocate, (3) reduce default NAT timeout from 60 s to 10 s for non-voice flows so DNS-style one-shot sockets release quickly. Voice flows still get 120 s. Cleanup interval also tightened from 10 s to 5 s.

title: Bind transport sockets to physical interface IP, not just IP_UNICAST_IF
description: 1.28.15 set IP_UNICAST_IF on the daemon's transport sockets but a profile from a 1.28.15 install showed the daemon's UDP packets were still entering bridgeInbound (5.6s in the self-bypass branch). Turns out IP_UNICAST_IF on connected UDP sockets is only "advisory" on some Windows builds — the kernel can still pick the TUN default route. Now we additionally bind the socket source IP via Dialer.LocalAddr to the physical interface address, which forces the kernel's source-address selection and routing decision. IP_UNICAST_IF is kept as belt-and-suspenders. Added a one-shot log line so the next bug shows local/remote/ifIndex of the actual socket.

title: UDP and TLS transports now use protected dial — bypass TUN routing
description: The proxy server transports were using net.DialUDP / tls.DialWithDialer directly, with no socket-level interface binding. Once the TUN engine added its default route, every outbound packet from the daemon's own proxy stream followed it — straight back into bridgeInbound. The new self-detection branch saw "this is our own packet" and reflected it through NAT bypass, doubling the work and burning CPU on a feedback loop. After the IP_UNICAST_IF byte-order fix in 1.28.14 the loop stopped reaching infinity, but the daemon still spent a lot of CPU on the bounce. Now both UDP and TLS transports go through protectedDialUDP, which binds the socket to the physical interface (Windows IP_UNICAST_IF / macOS IP_BOUND_IF). Loopback dials skip the binding so tests still pass.

title: Windows IP_UNICAST_IF byte order — protectedDial was silently ignored, daemon traffic looped through TUN
description: Microsoft requires the interface index in network byte order via htonl. The previous code used `uint32(ifIndex) << 16` which gave the wrong value (`0x00030000` for index 3 instead of the correct `0x03000000`). Windows silently ignored the bogus socket option and the "bypass" sockets followed the system default route — which in TUN mode is the TUN device. Daemon's own outbound TCP looped back into bridgeInbound. "All traffic" mode survived because the main proxy stream is opened once and multiplexed; "Selected" mode broke immediately because every isSelf bypass needed a fresh dial. Fix is `<< 24`, equivalent to htonl on little-endian.

title: bridgeOutbound treated io.EOF from Buffer.ReadAt as fatal — silenced reverse-direction packets since 1.28.10
description: gVisor's Buffer.ReadAt follows the io.ReaderAt convention: it returns io.EOF when the read reaches the end of the buffer, even on a successful FULL read. The 1.28.10 perf rewrite treated any non-nil err as fatal and returned, killing the bridgeOutbound goroutine after the very first outbound packet — silently, with no close log. Helper saw `daemon→TUN=0` forever, apps got no responses, D3 stall detector kept firing. Now we check the byte count instead and only bail on a genuine short read; the close path also gets explicit logging so the next silent exit can't hide.

title: Reentrancy guard in client startReconnect
description: The previous guard checked the React `reconnecting` state, which is async — when the polling effect and a transport-error effect both fired in the same tick, both saw `reconnecting=false`, both passed, both spawned independent retry loops. Result: multiple parallel /tun/start calls hitting the daemon back-to-back, each triggering "engine already active, restarting" and rebuilding gVisor stack and NAT tables. Now uses a synchronous ref so only one retry loop ever runs.

title: Goroutine stack trace on engine restart
description: Engine.Start now logs a stack trace when it has to stop a still-active engine before starting. Makes runaway client retry loops immediately diagnosable instead of guessing from the daemon log.

title: NAT readLoop holding aliased IP slices into a reused packet buffer
description: The 1.28.9 bridgeInbound buffer-reuse perf fix introduced a silent corruption: ParseIPv4Header returns srcIP/dstIP as net.IP slices into the caller's packet buffer, and NAT.HandlePacket was passing those slices directly to a goroutine that outlives the call. After bridgeInbound moved to the next packet, the goroutine's stored IPs became garbage — reply UDP packets (DNS responses, voice) were being built with bogus headers and dropped by Windows. Symptom: browsers fine (sites use SOCKS5, server-side resolve), but Telegram/Discord/anything depending on local DNS broken. Now both IPs are cloned before the readLoop spawns.

title: TUN bridgeOutbound — eliminate per-packet Flatten() allocation
description: gVisor's Buffer.Flatten() does an unconditional `make([]byte, 0, size)` internally, so calling it per outbound packet was generating one fresh slice per packet on the GC heap. Mirrors the bridgeInbound fix from 1.28.9: reuse a single growing frame buffer (length prefix + payload) across iterations and copy via ReadAt straight into it. Bonus: also coalesces the two conn.Write calls (length, then data) into a single Write — saves a syscall per packet on Windows.

title: TUN bridgeInbound — eliminate per-packet slice allocation
description: gVisor's buffer.MakeWithData copies the payload into its own pooled chunk, so the caller's slice is dead the instant InjectInbound returns. Pre-fix we made a fresh `[]byte` per inbound packet, generating one allocation per packet for GC to reap. Windows pprof showed ~70% CPU in runtime.gcDrain at idle. Now bridgeInbound reuses a single growing buffer across iterations (single-goroutine, no synchronization needed).

title: Windows daemon CPU — rate-limit procinfo cache miss refreshes
description: The Windows process-by-port lookup was forcing a full GetExtendedTcpTable/GetExtendedUdpTable scan on every cache miss. With browsers cycling ephemeral UDP source ports for DNS and other lookups, this thrashed the kernel scan. v1.28.7 pprof showed ~13% CPU still in refreshUDP. Now miss-driven refreshes are capped at one per 250ms per network — periodic 2s refresh still keeps the cache fresh, but new unknown ports return "unknown app" briefly instead of triggering an unbounded scan storm.

title: Windows daemon idle CPU burn — cache physical interface index
description: Windows CachePhysicalInterface was a no-op stub, so every protectedDial was calling net.Interfaces → GetAdaptersAddresses per connection. A pprof capture of an idle TUN-mode daemon showed ~28% CPU in that path plus another ~35% in GC cleaning up the allocations — a busy browser was burning 40-60% of one core on nothing but interface enumeration. Now mirrors the darwin implementation: detect once at engine.Start, cache, clear on Stop.

title: pprof endpoints on daemon for CPU profiling
description: Daemon now exposes /debug/pprof/* on its local 127.0.0.1:9090 API so we can capture CPU profiles to investigate Windows idle CPU burn. Curl /debug/pprof/profile?seconds=30 for a 30s CPU sample.

title: ARQ send buffer early-out on empty state
description: SendBuffer.Expired and AckCumulative skip their loops when the buffer is empty, cutting a bit of 20Hz × N-streams overhead in the UDP retransmit loop.

title: Document Windows window-handoff ordering rule and --trace flag in CLAUDE.md
Windows startup crash since 1.27 — loader-to-main window handoff
description: Create the main window before destroying the loader. The old order destroyed the loader first, which synchronously fired window-all-closed (zero windows for one tick) and on Windows that handler quits the app — the subsequent createWindow ran inside an already-quitting process and the main UI never appeared.

title: Diagnostic crash logger behind --trace flag
description: Launch with --trace (or SMUROV_DEBUG=1 on macOS) to enable boot traces and crash capture to ~/Desktop/smurov-crash.log for future troubleshooting.

title: Force diagnostic logger on for Windows debug cycle
description: Windows UAC elevation drops env vars so SMUROV_DEBUG=1 never reaches the elevated child, and --debug conflicts with Electron's legacy Node flag. Temporarily always-on until the 1.27+ Windows crash is found.

title: More diagnostic hooks for Windows startup crash
description: Added window lifecycle, webContents load/dom/crash/unresponsive, app quit-phase traces and console-message capture at all levels to pinpoint the silent exit after bootMainApp.

title: Landing page overhaul
description: Redesigned landing: hero, 6 feature cards (port 443, AutoTransport, hybrid TUN+Browser, split tunneling, Kill Switch, hardware-bound), benchmark table vs WireGuard and Outline, 3-step onboarding. EN/RU language switcher, admin panel link removed, download buttons auto-resolve to the latest GitHub release with proper Apple and Windows SVG logos.

title: Renderer crash diagnostics
description: Hooks render-process-gone, did-fail-load, preload-error and console-messages into the crash log so we can catch renderer-side startup failures too.

title: Diagnostic crash logger for Windows startup
description: Writes uncaught exceptions and boot-phase traces to ~/Desktop/smurov-crash.log to pinpoint where startup crashes happen on Windows.

title: Mode selector moved to header, traffic switch on main tab
description: Full (TUN) / Browser only toggle is now always visible in the title bar; All traffic / Selected apps switch lives inline with the Main tab.

title: Desktop notifications on connect, disconnect, and reconnect
D3 stall detector now rebuilds transport
description: Previously left tunnel wedged in Reconnecting state forever.

title: Extension collapses discovered hosts to eTLD+1
description: Reduces domain list size on SPAs with many subdomains.

title: Site catalog search in Add Site modal
description: Search the global catalog when adding sites — type to find, click cards to select, add multiple at once.

title: Auto-connect on cold start
description: Client now re-establishes the SOCKS5 tunnel automatically on launch when a device key is stored, instead of leaving the browser proxy off until you click Connect.

title: Daemon CORS for dev builds
description: Added CORS headers on public daemon endpoints so `make dev` renderer (http://localhost:5174) can actually reach the API on 127.0.0.1:9090.

title: Finish scanning button in the extension
description: The discovery panel now has a "Finish scanning" button to dismiss the scanner on sites where you're done adding domains.

title: Search on admin Sites page
description: Admin panel Sites table now has a search box that filters by label, primary domain, or slug.

title: Popup-расширение теперь панель управления для активной вкладки: проксировать сайт одним кликом, выключить проксирование, переключение применяется немедленно через автоматический reload вкладки. Daemon забрал на себя формирование PAC — refactor устраняет race conditions между переключениями из popup'а и из десктоп-клиента.
Bump desktop client version to 1.26.0 for popup control panel feature
Bump browser extension version to 0.2.0
Content script panel renders catalog_disabled state with Enable button
Add control panel layout styles to popup.css
Popup is now a per-tab control panel with 6 states (pairing, daemon_down, system_page, not_in_catalog, proxied, catalog_disabled)
Service worker refreshTabState now correctly maps catalog_disabled state and exposes popup handlers
Add setEnabled to extension daemon-client for popup proxy toggle

Sites mutations route through daemon HTTP — drop pendingOps queue and direct server sync

Drop sites field from setPacSites window declaration in useDaemon.ts
Expose 4 new daemon site IPC methods in preload and drop sites from setPacSites
Add IPC handlers for daemon site mutations and simplify pac-sites handler
Cache daemon token in memory in extension.ts to avoid disk reads on every IPC call
Wire RebuildPAC into sites cache-replace callback for automatic PAC updates
Daemon now owns the PAC domain list — handlePacSitesUpdate ignores sites field
Add GET /sites/my endpoint (no auth, localhost-only) for desktop renderer to pull cache
Add POST /sites/remove endpoint for browser extension and desktop client
Add POST /sites/set-enabled endpoint for browser extension and desktop client
Revert NewPacSites default and make TestRebuildPACSkipsCloseAllConnsWhenUnchanged self-contained
Add Server.RebuildPAC with diff guard to avoid 5-min connection resets
Add Manager.RemoveSite for per-user site deletion through server sync
Fix latent data race in TestManagerSetEnabledTogglesViaSync
Add Manager.SetEnabled for per-user enable/disable through server sync
Add SetOnCacheReplaced callback wiring to sites Manager
Add EnabledDomains helper to Manager
Vendor ExpandDomains from TypeScript to Go in daemon/internal/sites
План имплементации popup control panel: 33 task'а с TDD-разбивкой по 4 фазам — daemon (Go), desktop client (TS), browser extension (JS), E2E verification.

Спека popup control panel: применены правки независимого ревью — async mutation API, диф перед CloseAllConns, renderer pull'ится от daemon вместо сервера каталога, daemon token caching, migration plan для legacy pendingOps.
Спека popup'а как per-tab control panel для активной вкладки + минимальный refactor PAC ownership к daemon. Полная консолидация sites layer вынесена в backlog как отдельная инициатива.
Admin UI: страница Sites со списком и детальной страницей /admin/sites/{id} для модерации каталога
Admin API: GET /admin/api/sites, GET /admin/api/sites/{id}, DELETE /admin/api/sites/{id}, DELETE /admin/api/sites/{id}/domains/{domain}

AppRules.tsx: memoize siteDomains/liveSites/enabledSet — без этого applyPac useCallback и re-apply useEffect фаерились на каждом рендере, зовя networksetup десятки раз в секунду, что вешало macOS при запуске клиента
Extension Flow 3: detect top-level block → verify через /sites/test → banner с одним кликом
Extension Flow 2: failed sub-resource на проксируемом сайте → immediate discover
Extension discovery: webRequest hook → накопление доменов → батч flush в /sites/discover
POST /sites/test — HEAD-запрос через SOCKS5 тоннель для подтверждения блокировки
Extension README с install + usage инструкциями для sideload
Extension Flow 1: клик "Add to proxy" → /sites/add → state→discovering
Extension tab state machine — отслеживает текущий site и пушит state в content script
Extension popup: pairing screen + paired state с unpair кнопкой
Daemon HTTP API: GET /sites/match, POST /sites/add, POST /sites/discover
Контент-скрипт расширения с панелью на Shadow DOM
Extension service worker + daemon client wrapper с token storage

In-memory my_sites cache в демоне с subdomain matching
Вкладка "Browser Extension" в десктоп-клиенте с токеном для пейринга
Daemon SyncClient — HTTP клиент к серверному /api/sync для extension API

add_domain sync op для обогащения site_domains через discovery
Implementation plan для browser extension v1: 19 задач от server add_domain op до extension Flow 3 block detection

Спека browser extension v1: in-page панелька, auto-discovery через webRequest, block detection с silent verification
Каталог сайтов с синхронизацией: грид сайтов теперь синкается между устройствами через сервер

ApplyToggleOp (last-write-wins) и ApplyRemoveOp для sync-операций
ApplyAddOp с дедупликацией, slug collision handling и валидацией домена

Дизайн-спек: серверный каталог сайтов + per-user sync (этап A)
Окно реконнекта увеличено до ~60 сек (20 попыток × 3 сек) вместо 15 сек
TUN: детектор реконнекта по ошибкам OpenStream — срабатывает за ~5 сек вместо минуты, когда transport.Alive() врёт про живой транспорт
StatusBar: кнопка Disconnect остаётся кликабельной во время Reconnecting
App: индикатор Reconnecting от daemon мерджится в StatusBar
useDaemon: тип DaemonStatus расширен на статус reconnecting
TUN Engine: unit-тесты для state machine и kill switch
TUN: kill switch — отказ новых TCP/UDP потоков в Reconnecting
TUN Engine: Reconnecting статус с детекторами D1/D2
Tunnel: unit-тесты для state machine, детектора stall и kill switch
Tunnel: kill switch — отказ новых SOCKS5 запросов в Reconnecting
Tunnel: детекторы D1/D2/D3 для автоматического перехода в Reconnecting
Tunnel: новый статус Reconnecting с helper-методами для перехода
RateMeter: трекинг lastByteAt для детектора traffic stall
План имплементации: автоматический реконнект и kill switch
LIVE-индикаторы сайтов в браузере фейдятся в течение 5 секунд после прекращения трафика, а не залипают из-за idle HTTP/2 коннектов
Reconnect detection + kill switch design spec
LIVE indicators on browser site tiles — real active-connection tracking
description: The tunnel now tracks every in-flight SOCKS5 connection by hostname and exposes a /tunnel/active-hosts endpoint. The client polls it every 2 seconds and lights up a pulsing LIVE label on every site tile whose primary domain or related CDN domains currently have open connections. Works across the YouTube + googlevideo + ytimg set and similar tracker families via the existing RELATED_DOMAINS table.

title: Browser sites counter shows "all sites" instead of total count when All browsers is on
description: Previously "11 enabled" was confusing when the wildcard was active. Now it reads "all sites" when the wildcard is on, and "N enabled" with the real count of explicitly selected sites otherwise.

title: Daemon: per-host active-connection tracking in the tunnel
description: Simple concurrent map[string]int of in-flight SOCKS5 connections, incremented/decremented in handleSOCKS. Exposed via the new GET /tunnel/active-hosts endpoint.

title: Daemon deploys: server shutdown — tunnel now increments and decrements cleanly
description: handleSOCKS calls trackHost and untrackHost around the relay lifetime so the counter never leaks on errors.

title: Browser sites picker redesigned as a grid of brand tiles
description: Selected-apps mode now shows a 4-column grid of site tiles instead of the old collapsible list. The first tile is a wide "All browsers" card that toggles the wildcard proxy; when it's on, individual tiles dim to show they're overridden. Clicking any tile while All browsers is on switches to selected-sites mode and enables that tile. Tile colors come from the site's brand color, monochrome when disabled.

title: Add site modal with live favicon preview
description: Click "+ Add site" in the Browser sites section to open a modal. Type a domain and the preview tile updates in real time: Google's S2 favicons API is used for the icon, with a letter-avatar fallback (gradient background, hashed hue per domain) when no favicon is available. Esc or clicking the backdrop closes the modal.

title: Auto-update check now works on Windows — routed through system proxy via Electron net.fetch
description: The previous implementation used Node's fetch and https modules, neither of which respect the system proxy. On macOS the direct github.com fetch somehow squeaked through the TUN routes, but on Windows it failed with "Connection error" because GitHub is blocked at the ISP level in Russia. Switched both the version check and the installer download to Electron's net module, which uses Chromium's networking stack and routes through the SOCKS5 system proxy SmurovProxy sets up in TUN mode.

title: Redesigned status bar — wider window, inline Connect/Disconnect button, spinning globe indicator
description: Doubled window width (420 → 840) and merged the old StatusBar + full-width ConnectionButton into a single row: animated wireframe Earth with scrolling continents when connected, red outlined circle when disconnected, spinner during connect/reconnect. "Connected to <ip>" now has the IP in grey, uptime pushed to the right next to the action button.

title: Clickable transport badge to switch UDP/TLS/Auto on the fly
description: Clicking the UDP/TLS badge in the status bar opens a dropdown with all three transport modes and instantly switches — daemon setMode + forced reconnect. The old Transport submenu in Settings is gone.

title: Deploy workflow: pass commit message via env var to avoid bash parse error on multi-line messages
description: Previous commit directly interpolated the commit message into a bash expression, which broke when the message had newlines. Using an env var fixes that and also closes a shell injection vector.

title: Server graceful shutdown — broadcast MsgSessionClose to all UDP sessions on SIGTERM
description: Docker stop now gives the server 5s to send a new 0x07 MsgSessionClose packet to every active session before exiting. Daemon handles the message by closing the transport immediately, which triggers engine.healthLoop to reconnect within ~1s instead of waiting for the 20s keepalive dead-ticker. Cuts deploy downtime from ~4 minutes to ~2s for v1.24.3+ clients.

title: Deploy workflow: [skip-deploy] commit-message filter
description: Commits with [skip-deploy] in the message (docs, changelog, backlog updates) now skip the entire build/push/deploy pipeline so unrelated changes don't restart the production server.

title: Add backlog/ folder for deferred ideas
description: First entry: full graceful reload design (option C — SIGUSR2 fork-exec + FD inheritance + UDP session handover). Deferred until usage patterns justify the 4-7 day effort.

title: Fix dead UDP session after macOS sleep/wake — engine now detects transport death and reconnects automatically
description: Three fixes: AutoTransport now exposes DoneChan so the health loop sees UDP session death; healthCheck validates the actual active transport instead of a separate TCP/TLS dial; Electron client listens for powerMonitor 'resume' and forces a clean reconnect on wake.

title: Reduce CPU usage on Windows — retransmit interval 10ms→50ms, process table cache 500ms→2s
description: SendBuffer.Expired() now reuses buffer to avoid allocations on hot path.

title: Reduce log spam in bridgeInbound — log every 10000 packets instead of 100
Changelog UI: swap time and badges — time on left, badges on right
UDP dead timeout too aggressive (5s → 20s)
description: Two lost keepalive packets would kill the session. Now tolerates up to 6 lost packets.

title: Landing page download links now fetch latest release from GitHub API
description: Direct download URLs instead of broken local file scan.

title: Benchmark Test 10: SmurovProxy UDP vs TLS head-to-head on same server
Benchmark Test 9: SmurovProxy UDP v1.24.0 vs WireGuard head-to-head comparison
Auto-reconnect on transport loss
description: Daemon automatically retries connection up to 5 times when server restarts, no manual reconnect needed.

title: UDP device tracking in admin dashboard
description: UDP sessions now show as online connections with live speed in the admin panel.

title: Reduce maxRetransmits 50→15 to cut head-of-line blocking on lossy paths
description:
Rate-limit immediate ACKs (5ms min interval) to prevent ACK spam on out-of-order packets
Add 200ms time threshold for idle detection to prevent false STARTUP re-entry on brief pauses
Guard HandleData/HandleAck after Close() to prevent crash on freed structures

title: UDP throughput: download 0.7→5.0 MB/s, upload 1.0→5.0 MB/s — benchmark Test 8
Re-enter STARTUP on idle-to-bulk transition — fixes download regression on shared connections
description: Skip app-limited rounds in STARTUP exit check — prevents false exit during browsing

title: Prevent premature STARTUP exit — wait for BWE stability before checking bandwidth plateau
description: Fix pacer burstSize truncation causing ~33% underdelivery at sub-ms intervals

title: Defer pacing until BWE has enough samples to prevent early throttling
Add BBR-like STARTUP phase to congestion control
description: High pacing gain (2.885x) doubles sending rate each RTT until pipe is full, then transitions to steady-state 1.25x probing

title: Disable pacing during startup so BWE observes real pipe capacity
Use cwnd/RTT pacing during startup instead of BWE-based pacing
description: Prevents BWE-pacing feedback loop that throttled upload to 0.1 MB/s

title: Fix BWE undercounting acked bytes — count all packets from cumulative ACK, not just one
Replace CUBIC with BBR-like rate-based congestion control for UDP transport
description: cwnd now tracks actual delivery rate instead of reacting to packet loss — eliminates death spiral on lossy ISP paths

title: Server logs stored in SQLite (7-day retention) with admin panel Logs tab
description: All log.Printf output is captured to DB with auto-detected level (info/warn/error). Admin UI shows filterable, auto-refreshing log viewer.

title: Version bump to 1.23.26
Stabilize UDP throughput by capping cwnd and reducing loss aggressiveness
description: maxCwnd 1024→256 prevents overshooting channel capacity. cubicBeta 0.8→0.9 reduces cwnd cut per loss from 20% to 10%. Eliminates boom-bust oscillation (842→64).

title: Version bump to 1.23.25
Detect dead UDP sessions and disconnect within 5 seconds
description: Server now echoes keepalive packets. Client detects missing responses and auto-disconnects, fixing internet loss during server updates.

title: Version bump to 1.23.24
Raise UDP congestion control floor for higher throughput
description: minCwnd 32→64, initCwnd 64→128, maxBurst 8→16. Doubles minimum throughput from ~700 KB/s to ~1.4 MB/s at 60ms RTT.

title: Version bump to 1.23.23
Re-enable CUBIC cwnd reduction on loss to prevent congestion collapse
description: cwnd grew to 1024 and never decreased, overwhelming ISP buffers. Now reduces 20% per loss event (max once per 500ms) so CUBIC finds equilibrium.

title: Version bump to 1.23.22
Cap UDP burst size to prevent ISP buffer overflow
description: Uncapped burst of 17 packets at high cwnd overwhelmed ISP buffers causing congestion collapse (40 KB/s). Capped at 8 packets per burst.

title: Burst-based pacing to bypass macOS 1ms sleep granularity
description: Sub-ms pacing intervals now batch into bursts instead of sleeping per-packet, fixing throughput cap at ~1.3 MB/s.

title: Auto-restart daemon process if it crashes, preventing "Daemon not running" errors.
Raise UDP cwnd ceiling from 128 to 1024
description: Theoretical throughput cap goes from ~1 MB/s to ~9 MB/s on ISP paths with ~150ms RTT.

title: Remove cwnd reduction on packet loss for lossy ISP paths
description: Pacing prevents bursts; random UDP drops are not congestion signals. cwnd can now grow to maxCwnd=128 instead of collapsing to 32.

title: Add pacing + SACK-based loss recovery for UDP transport
description: Pacing spreads packet sends over time to avoid burst losses. SACK detection retransmits lost packets in ~1 RTT without cwnd reduction. Raised minCwnd 16→32.

title: Lower maxCwnd 512→128, maxRTO 2000→500ms, ssthresh=64 to prevent slow-start burst flooding ISP
Sort retransmit queue by PktNum (oldest first), add RTO to diagnostics
Tune UDP CC: raise minCwnd/maxCwnd, softer beta, longer recovery epoch, limit retransmit burst
Proper CUBIC ssthresh + lower maxCwnd to prevent UDP burst flooding
Revert CC to known-good version (initCwnd=128, beta=0.9, always-slow-start)
Replace fixed window with simple AIMD congestion control (always slow-start recovery)
Reduce fixed UDP window to 256 to match bandwidth-delay product and prevent burst flooding
Replace CUBIC congestion control with fixed window for UDP transport
description: Inner TCP handles CC; outer fixed window (1024) just provides ARQ reliability

title: Cap maxCwnd at 256 to prevent burst flooding, restore CUBIC with slow-start recovery at floor
Aggressive UDP congestion control: initCwnd 128, minCwnd 64, beta 0.9, always slow-start after loss
Fix cwnd stuck at minCwnd — reset to slow start when hitting the floor so cwnd can recover
Tune UDP congestion control for higher throughput (initCwnd 32, minCwnd 16, maxCwnd 2048, beta 0.8, recovery epoch 500ms)
